title: Java Memory Management
content:
### Memory Management in Java

Java has automatic memory management through garbage collection (GC).

---

### JVM Memory Model

```
JVM Memory
  |-- Heap (Objects, arrays)
  |     |-- Young Generation
  |     |     |-- Eden
  |     |     |-- Survivor S0
  |     |     |-- Survivor S1
  |     |-- Old Generation (Tenured)
  |-- Stack (Local variables, method calls)
  |-- Method Area (Class metadata, static variables)
  |-- Program Counter Register
  |-- Native Method Stack
```

---

### Heap Structure

| Region | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| Eden | New objects allocated here |
| Survivor S0/S1 | Objects surviving minor GC |
| Old Generation | Long-lived objects promoted here |
| Metaspace (Java 8+) | Class metadata (replaced PermGen) |

---

### Garbage Collection Process

1. **Minor GC** - Collects young generation
2. **Major GC** - Collects old generation
3. **Full GC** - Collects entire heap

**GC Algorithms:**
- Serial GC (single thread)
- Parallel GC (multithreaded)
- G1 GC (default since Java 9)
- ZGC (low-latency, Java 11+)

---

### Memory Leaks

Common causes of memory leaks:
- Unclosed resources (streams, connections)
- Static collections growing indefinitely
- Inner class references to outer class
- Uncleared ThreadLocal variables
- Listener/callback not unregistered

---

### JVM Tuning Flags

| Flag | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `-Xms512m` | Initial heap size |
| `-Xmx2g` | Maximum heap size |
| `-XX:+UseG1GC` | Use G1 GC |
| `-XX:+PrintGCDetails` | Print GC details |
| `-XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=256m` | Metaspace limit |

---

### Best Practices

- Nullify references when no longer needed
- Use try-with-resources for auto-closing
- Avoid creating objects in loops
- Use StringBuilder for string concatenation
- Use object pools for expensive objects
- Monitor with `jvisualvm`, `jconsole`, `jstat`

> **Key Point:** Java GC automatically frees memory from unreachable objects. An object becomes eligible for GC when there are no strong references pointing to it. Use `System.gc()` as a hint (not guaranteed).

quiz.question: Which region holds new object allocations?
When does an object become eligible for garbage collection?
